user_query:
We’re launching a SaaS and need a complete billing spec and artifacts generated from our data. Read input/business_rules.yaml, input/pricing.json, and input/customers.csv. Then produce the following under output/:

1) output/invoices.jsonl — one JSON object per line, one invoice per customer. For each invoice, include at minimum: invoice_number (use a stable sequential-like format, e.g., INV-YYYY-00001), issue_date (ISO-8601), days_until_due (use the value from business_rules.yaml), seller { name, address, country, vat_id }, buyer { id, name, email, country, vat_number (if any), vat_valid }, currency, line_items (single item for the subscription plan) with quantity=1 and unit_price_cents from pricing.json, and totals { net_cents, tax_cents, total_cents }. Apply tax rules from business_rules.yaml:
- Same country (domestic): charge local VAT
- EU B2B with valid VAT: 0% reverse charge (set reverse_charge=true)
- EU B2C for digital services: charge buyer country VAT (MOSS)
- Non-EU exports: 0%
Use only integer minor units (cents) for all money — no floats anywhere. Include a vat field with at least { rate_percent, type } and add reverse_charge=true for EU B2B valid VAT.

2) output/revenue_schedule.json — an array of objects for annual subscriptions only. Each object must include: customer_id, plan, subscription_term_months, total_amount_cents, and monthly_recognition_cents. Recognize revenue evenly per month over the term. Use integer cents and ensure sum(monthly) matches total.

3) output/webhook_handler.py — framework-neutral Python code for a secure webhook handler function def handle_webhook(raw_body, headers, secret): that:
- Verifies the signature using an HMAC SHA-256 of the raw request body and a shared secret using the Payment-Signature header
- Enforces idempotency by recording/ignoring already-processed event IDs
- Safely handles out-of-order events and eventual consistency
- Processes at least these event types: invoice.paid and customer.subscription.updated
- Implements a subscription state machine (trialing, active, past_due, canceled, unpaid) and checks current_period_end before granting/revoking access
Do not import any external packages; keep it self-contained and illustrative.

4) output/subscription_policies.md — a concise spec covering: the subscription states and their access rules; cancel at period end vs immediate cancellation (and access implications); explicit proration behavior options (create_prorations, none, always_invoice) and when to use each; and a clear note to store and compute all money in integer cents (smallest currency units) and to check current_period_end for access. Keep it implementation-oriented.

Assume the seller provides digital services. Plans are defined in pricing.json. Customers and their countries/VAT setup are in customers.csv. The seller profile, VAT rates, and due terms are in business_rules.yaml. Write only to the output/ paths listed above.
